themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over non-citizens in access to public services. This theme is evident in Spain, Italy, Sweden, France, Norway, and Argentina, where parties like Vox, Fratelli d'Italia, Sverigedemokraterna, Rassemblement National, and La Libertad Avanza are advocating for these measures, often sparking controversy and legal challenges.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox has implemented 'national priority' policies in regional governments, such as Zaragoza and Andalusia, prioritizing Spanish citizens in healthcare and social services. These policies have faced criticism from opposition parties and regional leaders.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: The Italian government, led by Fratelli d'Italia, has endorsed third-country migrant processing hubs, similar to the 'national priority' model. This approach has gained legitimacy through the Council of Europe's adoption of the Chisinau Declaration.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: The Sweden Democrats have faced backlash for distributing flyers falsely claiming that social services remove children from families unjustly, a narrative that aligns with their broader 'national priority' rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella of Rassemblement National has emphasized policies that prioritize French citizens, particularly in the context of immigration and national identity, aligning with the broader 'national priority' theme.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: The Progress Party in Norway has advocated for policies that prioritize Norwegian citizens in public services, reflecting a similar 'national priority' stance.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: While primarily focused on economic reforms, La Libertad Avanza has also engaged in debates over national priorities, particularly in the context of immigration and public resource allocation.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's policies and actions continue to dominate right-wing and far-right news coverage globally. This theme is particularly strong in the USA, Poland, Italy, Argentina, France, and the UK, where Trump's influence shapes political narratives, policy debates, and electoral strategies. His recent visit to China and his administration's stance on immigration, trade, and international relations have further amplified his presence in cross-national discourse.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's policies, including his approach to China, immigration, and trade, are central to right-wing discourse in the USA. His recent summit with Xi Jinping and his administration's actions on issues like border security and tariffs have been widely covered.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Poland's ruling party, Prawo i Sprawiedliwość, frequently references Trump's policies, particularly in the context of U.S.-Poland relations and NATO. The cancellation of a U.S. military deployment to Poland has been a major point of discussion.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties, including Fratelli d'Italia and Lega, often align with Trump's policies, particularly on issues like immigration and national sovereignty. Trump's influence is evident in Italy's approach to international relations and domestic policy.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Argentina's La Libertad Avanza, led by Javier Milei, has drawn comparisons to Trump's economic policies and anti-establishment rhetoric. Milei's alignment with Trump's approach to international relations, particularly with the U.S., has been a recurring theme.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Rassemblement National in France frequently references Trump's policies, particularly on immigration and national identity. Trump's influence is evident in the party's rhetoric and policy proposals.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, often echoes Trump's policies, particularly on immigration and national sovereignty. The party's rise in the UK has been linked to Trump's influence on right-wing politics globally.

themes.title: Corruption scandals rock far-right governments
themes.blurb: Corruption scandals are a recurring theme in far-right and right-wing governments, with recent coverage highlighting allegations in Argentina, Hungary, Poland, Spain, and the USA. These scandals often involve high-profile figures and have led to internal divisions, public backlash, and legal challenges, undermining the credibility of these governments.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Argentina's government, led by Javier Milei, is facing multiple corruption scandals, including allegations against Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni and other officials. These scandals have sparked public protests and legal investigations.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungary's new government, led by Péter Magyar, has launched investigations into alleged corruption under the previous Fidesz administration. These efforts include canceling high-value contracts and demanding accountability from former officials.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Poland's Prawo i Sprawiedliwość party has faced corruption allegations, particularly involving former officials like Zbigniew Ziobro. These scandals have contributed to internal divisions and public distrust.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox in Spain has faced criticism over its handling of corruption allegations, particularly in regional governments. The party's expulsion of co-founder Javier Ortega Smith has also raised questions about internal accountability.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: The Trump administration has faced multiple corruption allegations, including investigations into financial dealings and conflicts of interest. These scandals have been a recurring theme in U.S. political discourse.

themes.title: Far-right parties exploit immigration tensions
themes.blurb: Immigration remains a central issue for far-right and right-wing parties, with recent coverage highlighting how these parties are leveraging immigration tensions to mobilize support. This theme is evident in Spain, France, Germany, the UK, Norway, and Italy, where parties like Vox, Rassemblement National, Alternative für Deutschland, Reform UK, Fremskrittspartiet, and Fratelli d'Italia are using anti-immigration rhetoric to shape policy and electoral strategies.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox has intensified its anti-immigration rhetoric, particularly in the context of regional elections in Andalusia. The party's 'national priority' policies and opposition to migrant regularization have been key themes in its campaign.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Rassemblement National continues to focus on immigration as a central issue, with leaders like Jordan Bardella and Marine Le Pen advocating for stricter immigration controls and national priority policies.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: The AfD in Germany has made immigration a cornerstone of its platform, advocating for stricter asylum policies and deportation measures. The party's rising poll numbers reflect its success in mobilizing anti-immigration sentiment.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, has centered its platform on anti-immigration policies, including calls to ban foreign flags on public buildings and stricter border controls.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: The Progress Party in Norway has advocated for stricter immigration policies, particularly in the context of labor migration and asylum seekers.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties, including Fratelli d'Italia and Lega, have focused on immigration as a key issue, advocating for stricter border controls and third-country migrant processing hubs.

themes.title: Right-wing leaders face internal party divisions
themes.blurb: Internal divisions within right-wing and far-right parties are a recurring theme, with recent coverage highlighting conflicts in Poland, Spain, Italy, the UK, and Hungary. These divisions often revolve around leadership disputes, policy disagreements, and strategic differences, threatening the unity and electoral prospects of these parties.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Poland's Prawo i Sprawiedliwość party is facing significant internal divisions, particularly between factions led by Mateusz Morawiecki and Przemysław Czarnek. These conflicts have led to public disputes and weakened the party's cohesion.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox in Spain has experienced internal divisions, including the expulsion of co-founder Javier Ortega Smith. These conflicts have raised questions about the party's direction and leadership.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italy's right-wing coalition, including Fratelli d'Italia and Lega, has faced internal tensions over policy issues such as assisted suicide and migration. These divisions have complicated the government's ability to implement its agenda.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK has faced internal divisions, including the suspension and resignation of councillors over controversial statements and actions. These conflicts have raised questions about the party's stability and leadership.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Hungary's Fidesz party has experienced internal divisions following its election loss, with debates over the party's future direction and leadership. The new Tisza government has also faced challenges in maintaining unity.
